az-001.attackPerspective: Without MFA, a single stolen password grants full access to all Azure resources the user can reach. Password spray, credential stuffing, and phishing attacks succeed over 99% of the time when MFA is not enforced. Microsoft reports that MFA blocks 99.9% of automated account compromise attacks.
az-001.validationNotes:
# Verify CA policy exists and is enabled
Get-MgIdentityConditionalAccessPolicy | Where-Object { $_.DisplayName -like '*MFA*' } | Select DisplayName, State | Format-Table

# Check MFA registration completion
$report = Get-MgReportAuthenticationMethodUserRegistrationDetail -All
$total = $report.Count
$mfaEnabled = ($report | Where-Object { $_.IsMfaRegistered }).Count
Write-Host "MFA Registered: $mfaEnabled / $total ($([math]::Round($mfaEnabled/$total*100,1))%)"
az-001.tuningNotes: Deploy in report-only mode first to identify users who would be blocked. Exclude break-glass accounts but monitor them separately. Use Authentication Strengths to require phishing-resistant MFA (FIDO2/Windows Hello) for admins. Allow 'remember MFA for 14 days' on compliant devices to reduce user friction.
az-001.advancedDetection:
// KQL: Detect sign-ins without MFA
SigninLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(24h)
| where AuthenticationRequirement == 'singleFactorAuthentication'
| where ResultType == 0  // Successful
| where AppDisplayName !in ('Azure Portal', 'Microsoft Graph')  // Focus on cloud apps
| summarize Count = count() by UserPrincipalName, AppDisplayName
| sort by Count desc

az-005.attackPerspective: Microsoft Defender for Cloud provides the central security posture and threat detection layer for Azure. Without it, there's no unified view of misconfigurations, no vulnerability assessments for VMs, no threat alerts for storage/SQL/Key Vault, and no Secure Score tracking. Attackers specifically target subscriptions without monitoring.
az-005.implementationNotes:
# Check current Defender plans
Connect-AzAccount
Get-AzSecurityPricing | Format-Table Name, PricingTier, FreeTrialRemainingTime

# Enable all critical plans
$plans = @('VirtualMachines','SqlServers','AppServices','StorageAccounts','KeyVaults','Dns','Arm','Containers')
foreach ($plan in $plans) {
  Set-AzSecurityPricing -Name $plan -PricingTier 'Standard'
  Write-Host "Enabled Defender for $plan"
}

# Enable auto-provisioning of Log Analytics agent
Set-AzSecurityAutoProvisioningSetting -Name 'default' -EnableAutoProvision

# Check Secure Score
Get-AzSecuritySecureScore | Select DisplayName, CurrentScore, MaxScore, Percentage | Format-Table
az-005.validationNotes:
# Verify all plans are Standard
Get-AzSecurityPricing | Where-Object { $_.PricingTier -eq 'Free' }
# Expected: Empty (all should be Standard)

# Check active security alerts
Get-AzSecurityAlert | Where-Object { $_.Status -eq 'Active' } | Measure-Object

# Verify Secure Score
Get-AzSecuritySecureScore | Select Percentage
az-005.tuningNotes: Enable plans incrementally starting with VirtualMachines and StorageAccounts (highest ROI). Review Secure Score recommendations weekly — focus on Quick Wins (high impact, low effort). Suppress false positive alerts using suppression rules rather than disabling plans. Cost optimization: use Defender for Servers P1 ($5/server) for dev/test.
az-005.advancedDetection:
// KQL: Track Defender plan changes (potential tampering)
AzureActivity
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where OperationNameValue == 'Microsoft.Security/pricings/write'
| extend Tier = tostring(parse_json(Properties).resource.properties.pricingTier)
| extend PlanName = tostring(parse_json(Properties).resource.name)
| project TimeGenerated, Caller, PlanName, Tier, SubscriptionId
| where Tier == 'Free'
| extend AlertSeverity = 'High'
